title: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series
display_title: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series
wikitext:
[]: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by lmm34789 » Mon Aug 18, 2025 11:14 pm Basically the title of this post, the way I understand the ending of EoE is that Shinji and Asuka managed to reject human instrumentation and decided to embrace pain but also love, that's why Asuka caressed him at the end-an affirmation of the potential for their relationship. However, I find this in conflict with the fact that Shinji ended up with Mari in the Rebuild series , that way that 3.0+1.0 ended it seemed to suggest that Shinji and Asuka would not end up together and that Mari was his primary love interest and she respresented a new begining for him. Can, anyone help me understand what I interpret to be conflicting messages between the EoE and the Rebuild series?

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Aug 19, 2025 11:18 am You are trying to reconcile two different irreconcilable continuities. There was no Mari in the TV series/EOE continuity for Shinji to end up with. In the original show Asuka was his love interest all along so it made sense for them to end up together. There also was no 12 or 13 year time skip for Asuka to get involved with Kensuke and grow apart from Shinji. When you add on that Soryu and Shikinami aren't even necessarily the same person it makes it impossible to draw conclusions about the ending of EOE from the ending of Rebuild. It's apples and oranges.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by lmm34789 » Tue Aug 19, 2025 6:21 pm That makes sense, but the series are interrelated with the Rebuild being a continuity of the original series, in 3.0+1.0 the famous scene on the beachfrom EoE is recreated and Shinji said he used to have feelings for Asuka but not anymore.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Sun Aug 24, 2025 6:45 am lmm34789 wrote: Can, anyone help me understand what I interpret to be conflicting messages between the EoE and the Rebuild series?

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by mastafishere » Tue Sep 02, 2025 10:52 am I just wanted to respond to let you know, Weird_ocean that I appreciate your explanation and agree with your interpretation entirely. I don't mind that people didn't like Thrice, but it's annoying when they cite things in the movie that are their misinterpretation and misunderstanding (And this is not meant to throw any shade at OP who I respect for asking a genuine question). I think a lot of long time fans also unfairly criticize the Rebuilds because Thrice didn't fit in with the narrative they had built in their head which, to be fair to the critics, was because of how long of time there was between movies. I mean, what else were we supposed to do in that time besides speculate? On their own, I think the Rebuilds tell their own exciting story and have tremendous depth not seen in most media and get an unfair evaluation for not living up to the legendary original series (but I'm getting off topic here)

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Sep 03, 2025 1:42 pm I agree with what others have said. They're different stories with different themes. That said, I can't help but think that the overall message of "moving on" and "life is worth living" is present in both continuities. The people who worked on the films changed a lot between them. You could argue they matured in some ways. So, of course, one or two things would change. The way they see life itself may have changed.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Sep 03, 2025 5:53 pm mastafishere wrote: I think a lot of long time fans also unfairly criticize the Rebuilds because Thrice didn't fit in with the narrative they had built in their head which, to be fair to the critics, was because of how long of time there was between movies.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by mastafishere » Tue Sep 23, 2025 5:06 pm Weird_ocean wrote: I'm pretty much that person. I hate Shin Evangelion the most out of all EVA, but I also hate when people who praise it or talk about it, don't know anything about the story or lore. Most people get things wrong about these movies. But just because I hate it doesn't mean that I didn't try to understand it. I did. I figured it all out. Still hate it though. I hate it not because so many years past, I only watched them when every film came out, and I hated them not because I built some narrative in my head, but because they are just bad movies, form every possible angle.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by Weird_ocean » Wed Sep 24, 2025 3:01 pm mastafishere wrote: And I'm sure someone, possibly you, is annoyed that I wrote that and are about to tell me that I'm just empirically wrong .

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Sep 24, 2025 4:38 pm Hey guys, let's calm down. No need to point fingers at each other. I know these movies really mean a lot to us, but let's just be happy that there are still people with different opinions to talk to here.

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by mastafishere » Wed Sep 24, 2025 9:38 pm Weird_ocean wrote: mastafishere#945362 wrote: And I'm sure someone, possibly you, is annoyed that I wrote that and are about to tell me that I'm just empirically wrong .

[]: Re: How to reconcile the message in EoE with the ending of the Rebuild series             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Sep 25, 2025 2:38 pm I understand. As someone who often fluctuates between liking and disliking NTE, I've noticed how the discussion can get very personal at times, as with everything EVA-related to some extent. Right now, I think I have a neutral stance on the matter. I like some parts and dislike others. It's hard not to compare it to the original, but over the years, I've gotten over that.
